title: Multiply monomials
description: Express the product of two monomial expressions as a single monomial

assessment_items.question_content:
**Multiply.**  
*Your answer should be a monomial in standard form.*

${(3y^5)(5y^4)}=$ [[☃ expression 1]]
hints:
When a number is next to a variable, it means they are multiplied. So,

$(\blueD{3}\maroonD{y^5})(\blueD{5}\maroonD{y^4})$

is the same as

$(\blueD{3})(\maroonD{y^5})(\blueD{5})(\maroonD{y^4})$.

Now we can rearrange the factors because multiplication is *commutative* (a fancy way of saying that the order in which we multiply things doesn't matter).

$\blueD{(3)(5)}\maroonD{(y^5)(y^4)}$

Then simplify, and we're done!

$\blueD{15}\maroonD{y^9}$
